title: The Vein Reading Chamber

features.name: quartz-chip-left
features.desc-on-touch: a pale stone chip set into the board like a hardware component; if you place your hand on it, you feel the vein-patterns running through it, each one a path of meaning that has always been there, reading itself

features.name: vein-wireframe-tower
features.desc-on-touch: the veins of a stone rendered in three dimensions, each path branching like a circuit of decisions: which way the mineral could flow, which way it chose, which paths it abandoned; all visible at once in pale light

features.name: verification-pool
features.desc-on-touch: a pool of mineral solution that reflects nothing; you drop a question into it and the reflection in the non-reflection tells you whether the stone was ever there at all, or only believed to be

features.name: chip-right-pale-stone
features.desc-on-touch: another chip, this one quartz-pale and humming at a frequency slightly different from the first; together they form a question that the board is continuously asking itself about authenticity and layer

features.name: glyph-vein-archive
features.desc-on-touch: writing that follows the logic of stone: it branches, it converges, it disappears into layers you cannot reach; to read it is to learn what stone would say if it spoke, and to understand that it has always been speaking

inscription: the board reads the stone; the stone reads the board; the vein-paths between them multiply without division

file.name: Granite_Cross_Section_Spectral_Analysis_1987.txt
file.note: geological survey; final note by lead researcher reads: 'the samples became impossible to verify halfway through analysis. recommend repeating with different equipment. this stone should not be readab

exits.label: back down the spiral, toward the stone that taught us to wait
into the vein-systems where stone learns to root
deeper into the earth-board where circuits become bedrock
